title: Screen a drug-combination dataset

file.name: drug_combination_testing_data.xlsx
file.instrument: comboFM experimental validation set (Julkunen et al., Nat Commun 2020)
file.panel: MALME-3M melanoma (NCI-60), 6 combinations on 8x8 concentration grids

reasoning: Loading the combination screen. 6 drug pairs in MALME-3M melanoma, each an 8x8 dose-response matrix of percent inhibition.
I will fit single-agent responses, compute a synergy reference per pair, rank the combinations, and propose the strongest for wet-lab validation.

items.label: Analysis scope
items.chosen: Rank combinations by synergy and propose the strongest for validation

items.label: Single-agent model
items.chosen: Hill curve fit per drug (synergy package)

items.label: Ranking statistic
items.chosen: Mean excess over Bliss across the dose matrix

clarifications.question: What should I optimize for on this combination screen?
clarifications.context: 6 combinations were screened in MALME-3M melanoma on 8x8 dose grids. I can rank by synergy, fit parametric models, or focus on dose or mechanism.
clarifications.default: Rank combinations by synergy and propose the strongest for validation
options: Rank combinations by synergy and propose the strongest for validation
Full MuSyC parametric fit per combination

clarifications.question: Which synergy reference model should I use?
clarifications.context: Bliss independence assumes independent action; Loewe assumes dose additivity; HSA compares to the best single agent.

steps.title: Load combination screen and QC dose grids

summary: **6 combinations** screened in **MALME-3M melanoma** on 8x8 dose grids (comboFM, Julkunen et al. 2020).
**Everolimus + Romidepsin** is the strongest synergy (**Bliss +11.1**, HSA +11.2), concentrated in the high-dose region.
**Romidepsin + Vismodegib** is second (Bliss +9.4). Romidepsin (an HDAC inhibitor) drives the top two synergies.
**Dactinomycin + Romidepsin** is antagonistic (Bliss -6.5) and should be deprioritized.
**Recommendation:** advance Everolimus + Romidepsin to wet-lab confirmation.

figures.caption: Bliss synergy surfaces (excess over Bliss, % inhibition) for the top 3 combinations. Red = synergy.
Combinations ranked by mean excess over Bliss in MALME-3M. Teal = synergistic, red = antagonistic.
Single-agent dose-response for the top combination's drugs (Hill fits).

hypotheses.rationale: mTOR (everolimus) + HDAC (romidepsin) synergy is the strongest and dose-dependent, peaking at high romidepsin. HDAC inhibition plausibly sensitizes melanoma cells to mTOR blockade.
hypotheses.experiment: Confirm in a fresh MALME-3M 8x8 dose-response focused on the hotspot (romidepsin 100-1000 nM x everolimus 10-100 nM); extend to 2-3 additional melanoma lines to test generality; add a caspase/apoptosis readout to probe mechanism.

hypotheses.rationale: HDAC (romidepsin) + Hedgehog/SMO (vismodegib) is the second synergy; romidepsin recurs as a synergy driver.
hypotheses.experiment: Validate the combination and stratify by Hedgehog-pathway activity; test whether SMO inhibition is required for the effect.

hypotheses.rationale: Antagonistic in this line; combining these mechanisms may be counterproductive here.
hypotheses.experiment: Deprioritize; do not pursue without a mechanistic reason specific to another context.

sections.detailedAnswer: Across the 6 pairs, romidepsin (HDAC inhibitor) is the dominant synergy driver, forming the top two combinations with everolimus (mTOR) and vismodegib (Hedgehog/SMO). Everolimus + romidepsin shows the clearest dose-dependent Bliss excess, concentrated where both drugs are active, which is the signature of true interaction rather than a single-agent artifact. The single-agent curves show everolimus is weak alone while romidepsin is sigmoidal, consistent with romidepsin sensitizing cells to an otherwise modest mTOR effect.
sections.methods: Combination dose-response matrices from the comboFM experimental validation set (MALME-3M). Single-agent responses fit with Hill curves (synergy package). Bliss independence and HSA references computed per dose pair; synergy scored as mean excess over Bliss on the percent-inhibition scale (positive = synergistic). Reproducible from public/workbook/combo/pipeline.py.
sections.assumptionsNote: Small screen (6 combinations, one melanoma line). Scores depend on the reference model and dose-grid coverage; ranking is robust but absolute values shift under Loewe/ZIP. Findings are hypothesis-generating for wet-lab confirmation, not a validated result.
